meaning: n.[地](南美洲北部尤指安第斯山脉的)高山稀疏草地
examples: 900m (12,795) the Paramo, Ecuador: Highland communities here are having to adapt fast to the changes.
In Ecuador, where only 3% of the water supplies come directly from glaciers, 15% of the water is held in the boggy pasturelands called the Paramo.
The judges for the final round came from Tappan Street Partners, Longbow Research and Paramo Capital Partners.
Only yesterday a senior official at the European Central Bank, Jose Manuel Gonzalez-Paramo, declared that any restructuring would have "extreme adverse consequences".
